title: ulmus glabra var. pendula
intro: Ulmus glabra var. pendula, also known as the Weeping Elm, is a deciduous tree species characterized by its distinctive weeping branches and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The Weeping Elm is also valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antihelmintic to remove parasitic worms from the body. Ulmus glabra var. pendula can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
